sutta: buddha said monks these 8 worldly conditions obsess the world the world revolves around these 8 worldly conditions 1 a gain and loss fame and dishonor praise and blame happiness and suffering monks these 8 worldly conditions obsess the world and the world revolves around these 8 worldly conditions i'll just stop here for a moment these 8 conditions uh worldly worldly states early conditions roka dhamma and chinese they have in the chinese buddhism they are called the 8 winds 8 winds which move a person will shake the person [Music] and then the buddha continued monks to the unlearned common average folk come gain loss fame dishonor praise blame happiness suffering to the learned aryan disciple also come gain loss and so forth here then monks what is the distinction what is the peculiarity of and what is the difference between the aryan disciple and the common average vote god our doctrines have their foundation and the exalted 1 they are guided by the exalted 1 they have the exalted 1's protection well indeed worried for us if the exalted 1 would expound the meaning of his speech to us then when the monks have heard the exalted 1 they will treasure his words in his in their hearts and the buddha said then listen monks give heed i will speak yes lord they replied and the exalted 1 said monks gain comes to the unlearned common average folk who reflect not thus this gain which has come is impermanent painful and subject to change they know it not as it really is similarly lost comes fame dishonor praise blame happiness suffering they reflect not that such are impermanent painful and subject to change nor do they know these conditions as they really are gain loss and so forth take possession of their minds and wholes way there they welcome the gain which has arisen they rebel against loss they welcome the pain which has arisen they rebel against this honor they welcome the praise which has arisen they rebel against blame they welcome happiness which has arisen they rebelled against suffering thus given over to compliance and hostility they are not free from being born ageing dying sorrow lamentation pain grief and despair i say such folk are not free from ill but to the learned aren't disciple just the converse is the case and that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here these 8 conditions they all come to us we all experience them but an ordinary person when he experiences these things he's moved by them when what he considers is good now like gain comes he's very happy but when loss comes he's very sad and these things gain loss etc take possession of of his mind and moves his mind very much he's moved very much by likes and dislikes and and so because of that now you find this person most people ordinary people and they have this monkey mind who is always happy and sad and excited most of the time and the only way to overcome this like and dislike is in is is mentioned in the saka pandha sutra in the deega nikaya where sakha devaraja asked the buddha what is the cause of people quarreling and fighting in this world and the buddha said in gree and jealousy and then raja asked what is the cause of greed and jealousy and the buddha said i think i can dislike learning and the cause of life and dislike is desire and the cause of desire is thinking and the cause of thinking is proliferation of thoughts and how to stop the proliferation of thoughts and then the buddha said there's 2 types of happiness 1 is that 1 type of happiness is the happiness of thinking another type of happiness is the happiness that comes from not thinking and the the happiness that comes from not thinking is the happiness that is that comes from the jhana janik bliss when the person attains the second jhana upwards the mind stops thinking and that kind of happiness is superior to the normal happiness that comes from thinking people get a lot of happiness from thinking that's why some people like to day dream they like to fantasize uh they they they like to imagine and there's only a type of happiness which which is higher than this which is charming bliss and so the way to overcome these likes and dislikes is to attain the bliss of jhana so that then we can overcome the likes and dislikes the next utah is
